name: queries
context: CRUD query patterns, filters, pagination, aggregation, and sorting with Prisma Client
info: This sub-skill covers every query operation available in Prisma Client: reading data with `findUnique`, `findFirst`, `findMany`, `count`, `aggregate`, and `groupBy`; creating with `create` and `createMany`; updating with `update`, `updateMany`, and `upsert`; and deleting with `delete` and `deleteMany`. It details all filter operators (`equals`, `not`, `in`, `notIn`, `lt`, `gt`, `contains`, `startsWith`, `endsWith`, `AND`, `OR`, `NOT`), query options (`select`, `include`, `orderBy`, `take`, `skip`, `cursor`, `distinct`), and atomic number operations (`increment`, `decrement`, `multiply`, `divide`). See [[prisma/client/relations]] for relation-specific queries and [[prisma/client]] for setup.

sections.title: Read operations
sections.body: `findUnique` â€” by `@id` or `@unique` field, returns null or record. `findUniqueOrThrow` â€” same but throws `PrismaClientKnownRequestError` (P2025). `findFirst` â€” first matching record with optional `orderBy`. `findMany` â€” array of matching records with filters, sorting, pagination. `count` â€” integer count with optional filters. `aggregate` â€” `_avg`, `_sum`, `_min`, `_max`, `_count`. `groupBy` â€” group by fields with aggregate functions and `having` clause.

sections.title: Write operations
sections.body: `create` â€” insert one record with `data`. `createMany` â€” batch insert with `skipDuplicates` option. `update` â€” update one by unique field. `updateMany` â€” bulk update by filter. `upsert` â€” update-or-create with `where` + `update` + `create`. `delete` â€” remove one by unique field. `deleteMany` â€” bulk delete by filter (empty `{}` deletes all).

sections.title: Filter operators
sections.body: Comparison: `equals`, `not`, `in`, `notIn`, `lt`, `lte`, `gt`, `gte`. String: `contains`, `startsWith`, `endsWith` with optional `mode: 'insensitive'`. Compound: `AND: [...]`, `OR: [...]`, `NOT: { ... }`. Null: `where: { field: null }` for IS NULL. Scalar list: `has`, `hasEvery`, `hasSome`, `isEmpty`. JSON: `path` + `equals` for deep filtering.

sections.title: Pagination and sorting
sections.body: Offset pagination: `skip` + `take`. Cursor pagination: `cursor` + `take` + `skip: 1` â€” more efficient for large tables. Sorting: `orderBy: { field: 'asc' | 'desc' }` or array for multi-column sort. Order by relation count: `orderBy: { relation: { _count: 'desc' } }`. Use `distinct: ['field']` for unique values.

sections.title: Atomic operations
sections.body: Number fields support atomic updates: `increment`, `decrement`, `multiply`, `divide`, `set`. These are race-condition safe â€” they generate SQL like `SET views = views + 1` instead of reading-then-writing. Use for counters, stock levels, and any field requiring concurrent updates.
